paper_title: Fossil Reef Analysis on the Egyptian Coast of the Red Sea

methods.description: Process for collecting data from fossil reefs using Line Intercept Transects (LIT) to study the composition and structure of reef habitats.

steps.description: The localities were sampled during two field trips in February/March and November 2019.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct field trips to collect reef data

steps.description: The data were collected using Line Intercept Transects (LIT): a tape measure was laid out at a random starting point following the depth contour.
steps.searchable_summary: use LIT method to collect reef data

steps.description: The substrate underlying the tape was identified to an accuracy of one cm.
Corals were identified to genus level in the field or photographed and sampled for detailed identification in the laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: identify coral genus in field or lab

steps.description: Differentiate between rudstone, packstone, bafflestone, and framestone using the extended Dunham carbonate classification.
steps.searchable_summary: classify carbonate types using Dunham classification

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected reef data, including abundance calculations, transformations, and multivariate analyses.

steps.description: For corals that could not be identified to genus level, datapoints were removed prior to analysis.
The percent abundance of the categories sediment, Millepora, and other corals were calculated for each transect.
Mean percentages of Millepora, sediment, and the eight most abundant scleractinian genera were calculated for each transect.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mean percentages for reef categories

steps.description: After grouping the transects by Millepora presence/absence, the mean percent abundances and their 95% binomial confidence intervals were calculated.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mean percent abundances and confidence intervals

steps.description: Statistical analyses were performed with proportional relative abundances for each transect, which were square root transformed.
steps.searchable_summary: perform square root transformation on abundance data

steps.description: Nonmetric multidimensional scaling (NMDS) based on the Bray-Curtis dissimilarity was used to visualize differences between the predefined groups.
To test for differences between groups we used permutational multivariate analysis of variance (PERMANOVA).
Similarity percentages (Simper) were calculated to find the species contributing most to the dissimilarity between reefs with and without Millepora.
